description: Initial ClawCam ESP32-S3 camera target profile based on Espressif ESP32-S3-EYE v2.2 BSP camera and SD-card pin definitions. This profile is hardware-specific but not yet field-supported until a build, flash, capture, SD write, and sleep/wake test pass on physical hardware.

source_references.title: Espressif ESP32-S3-EYE Getting Started Guide
source_references.notes: Documents ESP32-S3-EYE v2.2 hardware, OV2640 camera, 8 MB Octal PSRAM, 8 MB flash, MicroSD slot, and battery pads.

source_references.title: Espressif ESP-BSP esp32_s3_eye.h
source_references.notes: Defines BSP_CAMERA_GPIO_XCLK, BSP_CAMERA_D0-D7, BSP_CAMERA_PCLK, BSP_CAMERA_VSYNC, BSP_CAMERA_HSYNC, BSP_I2C_SDA, BSP_I2C_SCL, and SD-card pins.

motion.notes: ESP32-S3-EYE does not include a built-in PIR sensor. Set this to an externally wired GPIO before enabling motion wake.

power.notes: Battery pads exist on ESP32-S3-EYE, but battery voltage measurement path must be verified before enabling ADC-based battery estimates.

promotion_requirements: ESP-IDF firmware builds with the esp32-camera component enabled.
Camera initializes and captures a valid JPEG frame on physical ESP32-S3-EYE v2.2 hardware.
Captured JPEG can be saved to the onboard MicroSD slot.
Gateway can ingest metadata generated from the hardware capture path.
Power and wake behavior are tested before enabling unattended field deployments.
